segments.s579-g33: نگشت is the past negative of گشت (here 'did not turn/deviate'); in Persian poetry the verb appears before its subject. آن دلاور is the subject 'that brave one/hero'. ز is the poetic preposition 'from', and پیمان خویش = 'his pledge' (خویش is the possessive formed from خوی + enclitic ش = 'his own').
segments.s579-g34: به مردی is a dative phrase 'to/into a man' (here 'to a man, into the care of a man'). نگه داشت is the compound past verb نگه‌داشت 'kept, entrusted' (noun نگه + auxiliary داشت) and takes سامان خویش ('his affairs/state') as its object. Note the enclitic ش on خوی forms the possessive 'his'.
segments.s579-g25: چو here introduces a temporal clause 'when'. The phrase ده سال گیتی همی داشت راست literally orders as 'ten years the world still had right' — همی + داشت is an older/perfective progressive sense meaning 'remained/was' (he maintained). راست is a predicate adjective 'upright/just' describing the state of the world.
segments.s579-g26: بخورد and ببخشید are past verbs describing two successive actions: 'he ate' (بخورد) and 'he gave/bestowed' (ببخشید). چیزی که خواست is a relative clause 'something that he wanted' (که introduces the clause; خواست is past tense 'he wanted').
segments.s579-g27: نجست is composed with an older negative prefix ن- + جست (جستن 'to seek'), so it means 'did not ask/seek'. از کسی marks the source/agent 'from anyone' (کسی here in negative context = 'anyone'). The following nouns باژ, ساو, خراج list the items not demanded; these are archaic/exaction terms (the exact meanings are lexicalized/archaic).
segments.s579-g28: همی + داشت again indicates a continuing state 'he used to have/hold'. رایگان 'free, for nothing' modifies what he possessed; آن گاه 'at that time/then' gives the temporal reference. The final و تاج links and names the crown/sovereignty he held.
segments.s579-g29: مر here is a dative/benefactive 'to me' (classical usage), so مر او را = 'to me, him (object)'. نکوکار is a predicate 'benefactor/virtuous person'. زان 'from that' (i.e., 'for that reason'). خواندند is past plural 'they called' (i.e., they gave him that epithet).
segments.s579-g30: که introduces the explanatory clause 'because/that': 'because whoever ...'. هرکس is 'whoever/anyone'; تن‌آسان is an adjectival compound meaning 'soft-bodied/pleasure-loving (luxurious)' describing those people; ازو 'from him/of him' and ماندند is past plural 'remained/stayed'. The clause explains why they called him نکوکار.
segments.s579-g31: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'. شاپور is the subject. گشت here means 'went/left/stepped down' (classical use of گشت = 'went away/turned' in context of leaving office). از در تاج و گاه literally 'from the door/seat of crown and throne' — در and گاه are used metonymically for the royal seat.
segments.s579-g32: مر again is dative 'to me'. او را is the definite object 'him/it' (here referring to the item). سپرد is the past 'entrusted/handed over' (transitive). آن خجسته کلاه 'that auspicious cap' is the object phrase; word order is determiner/adjective + noun as in classical Persian.
segments.s579-g1: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when' introducing a subordinate clause. بنشست is the past tense (3rd sing.) of 'to sit' (بنشستن); the clause places the action in the past. بر گاه شاه اردشیر is a prepositional phrase 'on the royal seat of Ardashir' (بر + گاه), where گاه here names the seat/place and شاه اردشیر is in apposition ('King Ardashir').
segments.s579-g2: بیاراست is a past (perfective) verb 'he adorned' (classical form with initial ب-). آن تخت شاپور پیر is the direct object placed after the verb: آن (that) + تخت (throne) + شاپور پیر (Shapur the elder) as a descriptive nominal phrase.
segments.s579-g3: کمر بست is an idiomatic past construction 'he girded his loins / prepared himself' (کمر = waist, بست = bound). و simply links clauses. ایرانیان را بخواند uses بخواند (past of خواندن with initial ب-) here meaning 'he summoned/called' and را marks 'ایرانیان' as the definite object.
segments.s579-g4: بر پایه تخت زرین نشاند is a result clause where بر پایه ('upon the pedestal/of the base') modifies where he seated them. تخت زرین = 'golden throne' (adj follows noun in Persian poetry), and نشاند is an archaic past transitive 'he seated (them)' (the object is the previously marked ایرانیان را).
segments.s579-g5: چنین گفت is a formulaic opening 'thus he said'. کز is a contracted form of که + از 'that/from', introducing the source phrase. دور چرخ بلند is a poetic genitive phrase 'the distant, lofty charkh' — here 'چرخ' refers to the heavens/sky (poetic) and بلند qualifies it as 'high/lofty.'
segments.s579-g6: نخواهم is first‑person negative 'I do not wish / I will not desire' used inside reported speech. که introduces the subordinate clause; باشد is the subjunctive/optative form 'be' (used after نخواهم to express the state wished against). کسی را گزند = 'that anyone [کسی] be harmed [گزند]' with را marking the patient; the whole clause means 'that no one be harmed.'
segments.s579-g7: جهان‌گر is the conditional sequence 'if the world...' where گر is the conditional particle attached to جهان (here tokenized as one word); شود رام = 'becomes tame/subdued' (شود = 'become', رام = 'tame'); با کام من = 'by my desire' or 'to my will' (با indicating instrument/means).
segments.s579-g8: ببینند is a subjunctive/optative or resultive form often used for 'they will see' in conditions like this. تیزی و آرام من lists two qualities 'my swiftness/keenness and my calmness' (adjectival/noun pair linked by و), with من as the possessor 'my.'
segments.s579-g17: سپارم is the speaker's verb in an older/poetic present or jussive sense 'I entrust' (سپار + ـم). بدو is the enclitic prepositional phrase 'to him' (به + او → بدو). The sequence 'گاه و تاج و سپاه' lists three objects joined by و in the normal Persian order (noun + و + noun); گاه here names the princely 'seat/office' and is the first item of the direct object. The whole segment is a verb + indirect object + coordinated direct objects construction.
segments.s579-g18: که introduces a subordinate/explanatory clause 'for/that'. پیمان is the noun 'pact/covenant' which is the object of کرد 'made' (کرد is the simple past 'made/did'). شاپور شاه is a name followed by the title (name + appositive title) placed after the verb for emphasis, a common poetic word order in classical Persian.
segments.s579-g19: Word order is subject (من) + object-marked noun phrase (این تخت را) followed by a nominal predicate (پایکار وی‌ام). را marks این تخت as definite/direct object, but poetically the predicate follows; پایکار is a nominal predicate meaning a claim/contestant/claim in older usage, and وی‌ام is a fused form (وی + ـام) meaning literally 'I am he' or 'I am his' — here functioning as the copular/pronominal predicate 'I am his'.
segments.s579-g20: همان is a demonstrative/adverb 'that/the same' linking this clause to the previous thought; از + پدر is a standard prepositional phrase 'from the father'; یادگار is a noun 'memento/keepsake' placed before the final predicate; وی‌ام again is the fused form (وی + ـام) 'I am he / I am his', so the clause reads as a nominal statement identifying the speaker with that keepsake.
segments.s579-g21: شما is the (plural/formal) subject. یکسره is an adverb modifying the verb phrase and means 'completely/altogether'. داد یاد آورید is an imperative/subjunctive construction: داد 'justice' + یاد 'remembrance' + آورید (آورد + ید) 'bring' — here آورید is a 2nd-person plural/imperative/subjunctive 'bring/restore'. The phrase means 'bring back/restore entirely the memory/remembrance of justice.'
segments.s579-g22: بکوشید is a 2nd-person plural imperative/subjunctive 'strive/try'. The coordination with و links the following command: آیین و داد آورید is 'bring/restore law/custom and justice' (آیین 'custom/law' + و + داد 'justice' as direct objects of آورید). The verb آورید repeats the same 2nd-person plural imperative as in the previous segment.
segments.s579-g23: چنان دان is an imperative construction 'consider/think it thus' (چنان 'thus' + دان 'consider/know' used as an imperative). که introduces the clause that follows. خوردیم here uses an older sense 'befell/happened to us' (خورد being used for 'befall'), and بر ما گذشت is a standard expression 'passed over/us it passed' where گذشت is the past 'passed'. The two verbs together describe events that happened to the speaker(s).
segments.s579-g24: چو here is a conjunction 'as/when/like'. مردی is used adverbially/qualitatively 'like a man' (or 'manly'); همه رنج ما is a noun phrase 'all our suffering' (همه 'all' + رنج 'suffering' + ما 'our'). باد is the optative particle ('may') used with the following verbal form گشت (from گشتن 'to become') to express a wish: 'may ... become.' Thus the clause is a blessing/wish: 'may all our suffering become (as) a man' i.e. 'may all our suffering be like a man's / be over like a man's.'
segments.s579-g9: ور is the conditional 'if' that begins the clause. ایدونک is an archaic/uncertain particle or name acting as the (implied) agent/subject here. با ما is the prepositional phrase 'with us' placed before the verb for emphasis. نسازد is a present/future-form of ساخت (here 'make/build') used in an optative/conditional sense ('does not/will not make'); جهان is the direct object 'the world' following the verb.
segments.s579-g10: بسازیم is the 1st-person-plural jussive/subjunctive ('we shall/may build' — here a firm future resolution). ما is the subject and follows the verb for emphasis. با جهان is instrumental 'with/by the world' and the final جهان is the direct object; the doubled جهان is a poetic/intensive repetition ('a world' or 'the world of worlds').
segments.s579-g11: برادر جهان ویژه is a noun phrase: برادر 'brother' with a genitive or epithet جانان/جهان and the adjective/epithet ویژه 'distinguished' following it. ما را is the clitic-object 'us' marked by را (here the direct object or emphasized element). سپرد is the past tense 'entrusted/handed over'. Word order places the noun phrase before the object-marked pronoun and the verb at the end, typical in classical Persian.
segments.s579-g12: ازیرا is an archaic/obscure connective (here functioning like 'for/therefore'); که introduces the explanatory clause 'because'. فرزند او is the noun phrase 'his son' (فرزند = child/descendant, او = his). بود is the past copula 'was'. خرد functions as predicate meaning 'wise' or 'of wisdom' (abstract noun used adjectivally). The whole clause gives the reason: 'for his son was wise.'
segments.s579-g13: فرستم is the 1st-person singular present/jussive of فرستادن ('I send' / 'I will send') used here to announce an action. روان is the direct object 'the soul/spirit'. ورا (vora) is the clitic meaning 'to him' or 'to there' attached after the object, and آفرین here functions as a proper name (a recipient) rather than the interjection — it receives the soul.
segments.s579-g14: که introduces a causal/explanatory clause 'because/that'. از is the preposition 'from/of'. بدسگالان is a plural noun 'the ill‑fated / ill‑omened ones'. بشست is the past form 'he washed/cleansed'. او is the 3rd-person pronoun 'he'. زمین is the object 'the earth/land'. Word order is standard: preposition + plural noun, verb, subject, and final object.
segments.s579-g15: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when/as'. The doubled شاپور شاپور is an emphatic or titular repetition (a common epic device: 'Shapur, the Shapur'). گردد is a present/future-form of گشتن/گردیدن meaning 'become' here. بلند is a predicate adjective 'high/lofty/great' placed after the verb.
segments.s579-g16: شود is another present/future auxiliary 'will be / will happen'. نزد او is the prepositional phrase 'in his presence / before him' (نزد = 'near, in the presence of'). گاه here functions as 'seat/place' (archaic use close to 'throne' in epic contexts) and تاج ارجمند is 'the esteemed/precious crown' (تاج = crown, ارجمند = esteemed). The phrase describes receiving a seat and a noble crown in his presence.

sentences.s579-s17: That brave one did not turn away from his pledge; he entrusted his affairs to a man.
sentences.s579-s13: When the world remained upright for ten years, he ate and gave away whatever he wished.
sentences.s579-s14: He demanded no baž, Sâv, or kharāj from anyone; he held the kingship and crown freely then.
sentences.s579-s15: For that reason they called him a benefactor to me, because whoever was pleasure-loving stayed with him.
sentences.s579-s16: When Shapur relinquished the throne and crown, he entrusted that auspicious cap to me.
sentences.s579-s1: When King Ardashir sat on the royal dais, he adorned that throne of old Shapur.
sentences.s579-s2: He girded his loins, summoned the Iranians, and seated them upon the pedestal of the golden throne.
sentences.s579-s3: Thus he said that from the distant high heavens he did not wish that anyone suffer harm.
sentences.s579-s4: If the world becomes subdued to my desire, they will see my swiftness and my calm.
sentences.s579-s9: I entrust to him the seat, the crown, and the army; for King Shapur made such a pact.
sentences.s579-s10: I am (his) for this throne; likewise I am a keepsake from his father.
sentences.s579-s11: You should wholly restore justice (to remembrance); strive, and restore custom and justice.
sentences.s579-s12: Consider it thus: what befell and passed over us — may all our suffering become like a man's.
sentences.s579-s5: If Aydunak will not make the world with us, we will make a world with the world.
sentences.s579-s6: He entrusted (or handed over) our distinguished brother of the world, for his son was wise.
sentences.s579-s7: I send the soul to Afarin, for he washed the earth of the ill‑omened.
sentences.s579-s8: When Shapur becomes great, he will receive in his presence a seat and the noble crown.
